information_density.rationale: Home exposes multiple navigation and comparison surfaces instead of a single hero block.

trust_clarity.rationale: Home is expected to route readers toward methodology, rankings, and representative examples.

internal_navigation.rationale: Navigation matrix and next-step modules should create obvious paths into page families.

serp_intent_match.rationale: Home usually serves broad brand and comparison intent, not a single long-tail need.

indexing_control.rationale: Home should always remain in the core indexed set.

risks: weak links into strongest page family
recommendedActions: Confirm the homepage routes users into the strongest ranking and detail surfaces.
Keep methodology and source-trust links visible above the fold or in the first scroll.

uniqueness.rationale: 3/3 sample entities have summaries and 3/3 have FAQs.

information_density.rationale: 3/3 sample entities expose metrics plus a narrative summary.

trust_clarity.rationale: Entity pages are expected to pair metrics with explanation instead of standalone scores.

internal_navigation.rationale: Entity detail section order contains 5 modules.

serp_intent_match.rationale: City detail pages usually serve the strongest long-tail comparison intent.

indexing_control.rationale: Entity pages need selective sitemap entry and family-level quality review, not full-surface indexing by default.

risks: too many similar detail pages entering the sitemap at once
recommendedActions: Audit a representative sample of cities for uniqueness, trust wording, and next-step links.
Keep the indexed cities pool smaller than the total published cities surface.

uniqueness.rationale: Region hubs gain uniqueness mainly from the entity mix and framing copy.

information_density.rationale: Category hub section order contains 3 modules.

trust_clarity.rationale: Region hubs should explain what qualifies an entity for that grouping.

internal_navigation.rationale: Region hubs are natural bridge pages between overview surfaces and detail pages.

serp_intent_match.rationale: Region comparisons often align with list-style or cluster-search intent.

indexing_control.rationale: Region hubs can usually remain fully indexed if the hub count stays controlled.

risks: hub pages that only repeat entity cards
recommendedActions: Give each region hub a clear qualification rule and why-it-exists explanation.
Make sure the hub links to a small set of strongest city detail pages rather than every possible candidate.

uniqueness.rationale: Ranking hub uniqueness comes from family labels, selection logic, and why each ranking exists.

information_density.rationale: Ranking hub section order contains 2 modules.

trust_clarity.rationale: Ranking hubs should explain representative vs full ordering and link to methodology.

internal_navigation.rationale: Ranking hubs are primary routing surfaces into detail and category pages.

serp_intent_match.rationale: Best/worst/comparison ranking intent is usually strong and legible.

indexing_control.rationale: Ranking hubs should always be in the core indexed surface.

risks: hub page that only lists links without framing
recommendedActions: Keep methodology and ranking-family explanations visible near the top of the page.
Use the ranking hub as an internal-link authority surface for the strongest detail pages.

uniqueness.rationale: Ranking detail pages need explanatory framing or they collapse into generic sorted tables.

information_density.rationale: Ranking detail section order contains 2 modules.

trust_clarity.rationale: Ranking details should make the ranking logic, exclusions, and tradeoffs explicit.

internal_navigation.rationale: Strong ranking pages should route readers into top detail pages and methodology.

serp_intent_match.rationale: Ranking detail pages often match specific list intent well.

indexing_control.rationale: A small set of ranking detail pages can usually remain fully indexed.

risks: thin ranking table with no rationale
duplicate ranking families with little differentiation
recommendedActions: Add clear framing about why the ranking exists and what it excludes.
Link ranking winners and notable outliers into the detail-page family.

backlog.action: Audit a representative sample of cities for uniqueness, trust wording, and next-step links.
Give each region hub a clear qualification rule and why-it-exists explanation.
Add clear framing about why the ranking exists and what it excludes.
